mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate below 0.5% signals a zero-interest-rate policy regime that compresses discount rates and forces capital into risk assets, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the immediate 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns are negative in > 55% of instances where FEDFUNDS < 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 7.0% indicates a weak labor market that suppresses consumer spending and corporate earnings, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC for UNRATE is positive (> 0.02) over any 3-year rolling window when UNRATE > 7.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts signal stabilization in the housing sector and increased construction spending, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the 20-day horizon regardless of other macro conditions.
failure_condition: HOUST shows no statistically significant correlation (p > 0.10) with 20-day forward returns in a bivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation (CPIAUCSL) combined with a static federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) reduces real interest rates, making equities more attractive relative to bonds and driving positive S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The interaction term between CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS has a negative coefficient in a multivariate model predicting 20-day returns.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production below 98.0 indicates weak manufacturing demand and potential recessionary pressures, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in > 65% of months where INDPRO < 98.0.

mechanism_narrative: Increasing non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects a strengthening labor market that supports consumer confidence and spending, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows a negative or null relationship with 20-day forward returns in a walk-forward analysis.

mechanism_narrative: Positive GDP growth (GDPC1) confirms an economic expansion regime where corporate earnings are rising, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are negative in > 50% of quarters where GDPC1 > 13000.

mechanism_narrative: A personal savings rate (RSAFS) above 400,000 indicates high consumer caution and reduced spending, which suppresses corporate revenues and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC for RSAFS is positive (> 0.02) over any 2-year rolling window when RSAFS > 400,000.
